title: tephroseris papposa subsp. wagneri
intro: Tephroseris papposa subsp. wagneri, also known as Wagner's Tephroseris, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Tephroseris papposa subsp. wagneri include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a popular herbal remedy in folk medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
